ip_outcome_note: No IP flag. No suspected_ip in frontmatter.

trivia_bullets.text: Battle for Zendikar (October 2, 2015) covers the second Eldrazi invasion of the plane, with the Zendikari resistance — vampires under Drana, roilmages under Noyan Dar, ground troops under Tazri, goblins, kor, and elven skyriders — converging against Ulamog under Gideon Jura's leadership. Unified Front's Converge mechanic and its production of Kor Ally tokens is a direct mechanical encoding of this multiracial coalition: mana from multiple colors represents the many factions literally unifying. The flavor text 'The kor's pride in their independence has given way to a strong sense of community that embraces all of Zendikar' is the kor's narrative arc across the Zendikar blocks in capsule form. [Scryfall: BFZ-53 flavor_text; magic.wizards.com: Battle for Zendikar Story Summary]
sources: magic.wizards.com: Battle for Zendikar Story Summary (2015-12-23)

trivia_bullets.text: Converge is an ability word introduced in Battle for Zendikar that rewards spending mana of multiple colors. Mark Rosewater later assessed Converge as 'a mechanic in the wrong environment' — it would have performed better in a block where color (as opposed to colorlessness) was the central tension, rather than BFZ where the Eldrazi's Devoid / colorless theme dominated. The mechanic had its best-supported design space in multicolor-heavy decks, not the inward-color-versus-colorlessness set. [magic.wizards.com: Storm Scale: Zendikar and Battle for Zendikar, 2016-11-21; magic.wizards.com: Battle for Zendikar Mechanics]
sources: magic.wizards.com: Storm Scale: Zendikar and Battle for Zendikar (2016-11-21)
magic.wizards.com: Battle for Zendikar Mechanics feature

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 19881 confirms Unified Front sees narrow but defined Commander play — primarily in Ally kindred decks. In Tazri, Beacon of Unity lists the card has 35% inclusion with +34% synergy; in Rukarumel, Biologist ally lists it hits 32% with +34% synergy. Generating four Kor Ally tokens at Converge-5 triggers multiple on-enter payoffs simultaneously (Kazandu Blademaster, Sea Gate Loremaster, Kabira Evangel), making it a niche but effective mass-trigger spell in 5-color Ally builds. [Scryfall: BFZ-53 edhrec_rank field; EDHREC: Top 10 Most Played Converge Cards in Commander]
sources: EDHREC: Top 10 Most Played Converge Cards in Commander (via WebSearch)

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Dan Murayama Scott. The card is legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander; not reprinted as of the Scryfall data snapshot. [Scryfall: BFZ-53 legalities + artist field]
